title: Programming Foundations
workflows: command line navigation
notebook-based analysis
IDE-based development
experiment replication pipeline
deliverables: group project: Python Implementation for Operational Management Experiments
business_framing: operational management experiment replication, supply chain research
learning_summary: Built Python scripting and OOP fundamentals, applying data wrangling with pandas and linear regression inside Jupyter notebooks.
review_questions: What is the difference between a class and an instance in OOP?
How do you use pandas to clean and reshape a dataset?
When would you choose a Jupyter notebook over a Python script?
What is experimental replication methodology in a research context?

title: Foundation of Professional Analytics
role_in_degree: core — integration + agentic AI + applied research
format: mixed schedule: 4 weekend agentic AI modules + open data sessions
theories: reasoning and planning in AI systems
workflows: Module I: What is an AI Agent + Good-to-Know Foundations
Module II: Building Agents with LangChain + Middleware + Advanced LangChain
Module III: Intro to LangGraph + Advanced LangGraph
Module IV: Frontier Topics + System-Level Design + Deep Agents
Open Data: research question → data cleaning → methodology → peer evaluation → results → white paper

deliverables.name: NS Health Data Project (Open Data)
deliverables.details: white paper on NS Health open data

cadence: mixed: weekend AI modules + biweekly open data sessions, Jan 7 – Apr 8
business_framing: enterprise AI adoption, public health analytics for Nova Scotia stakeholders, transparent code and replication
dual_pillar_description: Two parallel tracks: (1) Agentic AI Systems — LangChain/LangGraph pipeline design; (2) Open Data White Paper — NS Health data public utility research
learning_summary: Designed and deployed LLM-powered AI agents using LangChain and LangGraph, with governance frameworks and human-in-the-loop control for safe agentic systems.
review_questions: What is the ReAct agent architecture?
How do LangGraph interrupts work?
What is the difference between an agent and a workflow?
What does HITL control mean in agentic AI?
How does LangSmith support agent observability?

title: Predictive Analytics / Data Analytics
workflows: EDA → model building → significance testing → model quality → train-test → prediction intervals
business_framing: housing price prediction, insurance analytics, demand forecasting, financial planning
learning_summary: Applied multiple linear regression and statistical inference for business prediction, including train-test splits, F-tests, and prediction intervals in R.
review_questions: What is the difference between a prediction interval and a confidence interval?
How do you interpret a standardized coefficient?
When does an F-test fail to detect model significance?
What does it mean for a model to generalize well to new data?

title: Optimization & Decision Analysis
workflows: problem statement → decision variables → objective function → constraints → non-negativity → solve → sensitivity analysis → shadow price interpretation
business_framing: production planning, portfolio optimization, healthcare routing, supply chain, textile manufacturing
evidence_origin: [LOCAL COURSE ARTIFACT EVIDENCE] — no uploaded outline, using exam prep + assignment briefs
provisional_note: User-Confirmed Reconstruction — no uploaded course outline. Evidence: exam prep + assignment briefs.
learning_summary: Formulated and solved LP and IP problems using Excel Solver and Gurobi, including duality, shadow prices, sensitivity analysis, and decision analysis under uncertainty.
review_questions: How do I formulate an LP problem from a word problem?
What does a shadow price mean, and when is it zero?
When should I use integer programming instead of LP?
How does EVPI relate to decision tree analysis?
What is the primal-dual relationship in LP?

title: Enterprise Database Management
professor_note: User-confirmed: Hai Wang taught MBAN 5550 and supervised the Cruise Line Database Project
workflows: business problem → ER diagram → table creation → KPI queries → data expansion → issue identification → managerial insights
deliverables: 2 group projects (Cruise Line Database Project — multi-table relational schema, Power Query ETL, Power BI dashboard)
business_framing: cruise line reservation analytics, business intelligence from operational databases
learning_summary: Designed relational databases using ER diagrams and normalization, then queried them with SQL to compute KPIs and produce business intelligence.
review_questions: What is the difference between 2NF and 3NF normalization?
How does an ER diagram translate to a relational schema?
When would you use a JOIN vs a subquery?
What makes a KPI computationally efficient to query at scale?

title: Machine Learning & Artificial Intelligence
workflows: Wk 1-2: stats review → bias-variance → logistic regression → kernel → splines → Naive Bayes
Wk 3-4: kNN → grid search → CV → K-means → DBSCAN → hierarchical clustering
Wk 5-6: CART → bagging → RF → GBM → AdaBoost.M5 → XGBoost
Wk 7-8: SVM → neural networks → gradient descent variants
Wk 9-10: sequential forecasting → CNN → RNN → LSTM → Hugging Face
Wk 10-11: NLP → word2vec → transformers → fine-tuning
Final week: project development + final project
business_framing: applied ML across regression, classification, clustering, NLP, computer vision, forecasting

textbooks.title: Machine Learning Toolbox for Social Scientists
textbooks.author: Yigit Aydede

learning_summary: Applied machine learning from regression through deep learning and NLP, with rigorous validation using cross-validation and bootstrap across the full supervised and unsupervised toolkit.
review_questions: What is the bias-variance tradeoff?
How does random forest differ from boosting?
When should I use kNN vs logistic regression?
What is feature leakage and why does it invalidate a model?
How do you choose k in k-fold cross-validation?

title: Accounting & Financial Analytics
methods: NLP sentiment analysis on capital market data
workflows: Part A: Excel-based risk/uncertainty → financial statements → valuation
Part B: Python visualization → pandas analytics → big data mining → trading algorithms
Part C: NLP on financial text → ML forecasting → event studies
Equity research: traditional analysis → AI-assisted analysis → critical evaluation of AI outputs
governance_elements: AI as analytical assistant NOT decision-maker
cadence: weekly lectures + 4 assignments + group project + exam
business_framing: equity research, financial statement analysis, capital market analytics, investment recommendation
learning_summary: Applied Monte Carlo simulation, decision trees, and ML forecasting to financial analytics including pro forma valuation, event studies, and equity research white papers.
review_questions: When would you use Monte Carlo simulation instead of a single point estimate?
How does sensitivity analysis differ from scenario analysis?
What makes an equity research methodology academically defensible?
How do you apply NLP sentiment analysis to capital market data?

title: Ethics, Governance & Responsible AI
workflows: choose scenario → defend uncomfortable position → analyze own reasoning → video reflection
business_framing: corporate compliance, professional codes of ethics, reputational risk management
learning_summary: Analysed ethical frameworks (Aristotelian, Kantian, social contract) and applied them to AI governance, corporate compliance, and motivated reasoning in analytics decisions.
review_questions: What is the difference between virtue ethics and deontological ethics?
How does motivated reasoning create bias in analytics decisions?
What governance frameworks apply to predictive policing or healthcare AI?
What does Sarbanes-Oxley require of analytics professionals?
